# services.lemmy

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.lemmy`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.lemmy.adminPasswordFile` | `null or absolute path` | File which contains the value of `setup.admin_password`. |
| `services.lemmy.caddy.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable exposing lemmy with the caddy reverse proxy. |
| `services.lemmy.database.createLocally` | `boolean` | Whether to enable creation of database on the instance. |
| `services.lemmy.database.uri` | `null or string` | The connection URI to use. Takes priority over the configuration file if set. |
| `services.lemmy.database.uriFile` | `null or absolute path` | File which contains the database uri. |
| `services.lemmy.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable lemmy a federated alternative to reddit in rust. |
| `services.lemmy.nginx.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable exposing lemmy with the nginx reverse proxy. |
| `services.lemmy.pictrsApiKeyFile` | `null or absolute path` | File which contains the value of `pictrs.api_key`. |
| `services.lemmy.server.package` | `package` | The lemmy-server package to use. |
| `services.lemmy.settings` | `open submodule of (JSON value)` | Lemmy configuration |
| `services.lemmy.settings.captcha.difficulty` | `one of "easy", "medium", "hard"` | The difficultly of the captcha to solve. |
| `services.lemmy.settings.captcha.enabled` | `boolean` | Enable Captcha. |
| `services.lemmy.settings.hostname` | `string` | The domain name of your instance (eg 'lemmy.ml'). |
| `services.lemmy.settings.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Port where lemmy should listen for incoming requests. |
| `services.lemmy.smtpPasswordFile` | `null or absolute path` | File which contains the value of `email.smtp_password`. |
| `services.lemmy.ui.package` | `package` | The lemmy-ui package to use. |
| `services.lemmy.ui.port` | `16 bit unsigned integer; between 0 and 65535 (both inclusive)` | Port where lemmy-ui should listen for incoming requests. |
